settings.title: The People Who Actually Hold Your Keys
settings.excerpt: Trusting someone with a key to your home is no small thing, so here is who that someone is.
settings.body:
Letting a cleaner into your home is an act of trust, and we never take it lightly. So it seems only fair you know who our teams are and how we choose them.

We work in steady pairs rather than sending whoever is free. Most of our clients see the same two faces every week, which is how Maria and Joanna have ended up knowing exactly which mug a regular likes left on the draining board. That familiarity is not a nice extra. It is the whole point.

How we vet our teams

Everyone on the team is vetted, insured, and trained on our methods before they set foot in a client's home alone. New cleaners shadow an experienced pair for their first weeks, so by the time they work independently they already know our standards and your kind of home.

  • Background checks before anyone starts
  • Full insurance on every visit
  • A shadowing period before working alone

Keeping pairs together also means problems get spotted. The team that cleans your home each week notices the dripping tap, the loose handle, or the patch of damp long before it becomes a job, and they will mention it to you. A rotating cast of strangers never builds that kind of eye for a place.

If your usual pair is ever off, we tell you in advance who is coming instead. No strangers turning up unannounced, ever. You will always have a name and a heads-up before the doorbell rings.
